agent_output: {"handoff_brief":{"recipient":"Shaq","task_context":"Implement a pure TypeScript function `recalculatePrice(quantity: number, basePrice: number): number` that applies bulk discount logic and enforces a $0.01 price floor. This function serves a high-traffic pricing endpoint (~500 req/sec peak) but involves no I/O or external dependencies — it is stateless, deterministic, and suitable for inline execution. Deliver the function with a full unit test suite and JSDoc documentation.","domain_rules":[{"rule":"Final price = base price minus applicable discounts, floored at $0.01. Price can never be zero or negative.","testable_assertion":"For any input combination, recalculatePrice always returns a value >= 0.01"},{"rule":"Bulk discount of 10% applies when quantity >= 10.","testable_assertion":"recalculatePrice(10, X) returns X * 0.9 (unless floored), and recalculatePrice(9, X) returns X unchanged (unless floored)"},{"rule":"The $0.01 floor applies after all discounts are calculated.","testable_assertion":"If basePrice * (1 - discountRate) <= 0, the function returns 0.01"}],"architecture_guidance":[{"decision":"Pure TypeScript function, no classes or external dependencies","implementation_note":"Implement as a single exported function: `recalculatePrice(quantity: number, basePrice: number): number`. No state, no side effects, no DI needed."},{"decision":"Add JSDoc comment documenting the $0.01 floor behavior","implementation_note":"On-call engineers need to understand why a price of $0.01 can appear. The JSDoc should explicitly state: 'Returns a minimum of $0.01 when discounts would reduce the price to zero or below.'"},{"decision":"Unit tests only — no integration tests required","implementation_note":"The function is pure with no I/O. A comprehensive unit test suite covering the acceptance criteria plus edge cases is sufficient."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"criterion":"Bulk discount applied at quantity threshold","given":"quantity=10, basePrice=5.00","when":"recalculatePrice is called","then":"returns 4.50"},{"criterion":"No discount below quantity threshold","given":"quantity=9, basePrice=5.00","when":"recalculatePrice is called","then":"returns 5.00"},{"criterion":"Price floor enforced when discount would zero out price","given":"a discount that would reduce price to $0.00 or below","when":"recalculatePrice is called","then":"returns 0.01"}],"terminology_alignment":[{"domain_term":"Bulk discount","technical_term":"10% rate applied when quantity >= 10","definition":"A percentage reduction on base price triggered by meeting a minimum quantity threshold. Currently a single tier: 10% off at qty >= 10."},{"domain_term":"Price floor","technical_term":"Math.max(computedPrice, 0.01)","definition":"The minimum allowable final price ($0.01). Applied as the last step after all discount calculations."}],"contradictions_resolved":[],"open_questions":[]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":97,"high_confidence_areas":["Domain rules are unambiguous with concrete numeric examples","Architecture is simple — pure function, no design tradeoffs","All three agents are fully aligned on scope and approach"],"low_confidence_areas":["Only one discount tier is specified; future tiers may change the function signature but are out of scope for now"],"assumptions":["basePrice is always provided as a positive number (no validation of negative basePrice specified by any agent)","Rounding behavior for fractional cents is not specified — standard floating-point or two-decimal rounding may be needed at Shaq's discretion"]}}
